text: abhayam sattvasamsuddhir jnanayogavyavasthitih daanam damas ca yajnas ca svadhyayas tapas arjavam
translation: Fearlessness, purity of mind, steadfastness in spiritual knowledge, charity, control of the senses, performance of sacrifice, study of sacred scriptures, austerity, and straightforwardness.
significance: Divine qualities (Daivi Sampat) related to personal development.

text: ahimsa satyam akrodhas tyagah santir apaisunam daya bhutesv aloluptvam maardavam hriir acapalam
translation: Non-violence, truthfulness, freedom from anger, renunciation, peacefulness, aversion to fault-finding, compassion for all living beings, freedom from covetousness, gentleness, modesty, and steadiness.
significance: Divine qualities (Daivi Sampat) that maintain interpersonal relationships.

text: tejah ksama dhrtih saucam adroho naatimanita bhavanti sampadam daivim abhijatasya bharata
translation: Vigor, forgiveness, fortitude, cleanliness, freedom from malice, and absence of pride. These are the qualities of those born with divine nature, O descendant of Bharata.
significance: Divine qualities (Daivi Sampat) needed to restore strained relationships.

text: dambho darpo'bhimanas ca krodhah parusyam eva ca ajnanan cabhijatasya partha sampadam asurim
translation: Hypocrisy, pride, self-conceit, anger, rudeness, and ignorance—these, O Partha, belong to those born with a demoniacal nature.
significance: demoniacal qualities (Asuri Sampat) that lead to self-destruction.

text: daivi sampad vimoksaya nibandhayasuri mata ma sucah sampadam daivim abhijato'si pandava
translation: The divine qualities lead to liberation, whereas the demoniacal qualities lead to bondage. Do not grieve, O Pandava, for you are born with divine qualities.
significance: The two paths of humanity: liberation versus bondage.

text: dvau bhutasargau loke'smin daiva aasura eva ca daivo vistarashah proktah aasuram partha me shrinu
translation: There are two kinds of created beings in this world—the divine and the demoniacal. The divine has been described at length. Hear now from Me about the demoniacal, O Partha.
significance: The classification of humanity into two distinct spiritual paths.

text: pravrittim ca nivrittim ca jana na vidur aasuraah na shaucham napi chacharo na satyam teshu vidyate
translation: Those of demoniacal nature do not know what actions are right to perform and what actions are right to avoid. Neither purity, nor good conduct, nor truthfulness is found in them.
significance: Lack of moral discrimination as the starting point of moral decay.

text: asatyam apratishtham te jagad aahur aneeshvaram aparaspara sambhutam kim anyat kama haitukam
translation: They say the world is without truth, without moral basis, and without a God. It is brought about by union of male and female, with lust as its cause and nothing else.
significance: The godless, materialistic worldview driven purely by sense gratification.

text: etaam drishtim avashtabhya nashtaatmano'lpabuddhayah prabhavanty ugra-karmanah kshayaaya jagato'hitaah
translation: Holding on to this view, these depraved characters of little intellect, come forth with their fierce deeds, set to destroy the world by their harmfulness.
significance: The consequences of a purely godless worldview: destruction of self and society.

text: kaamam aashritya dushpooram dambha-maana-mada-anvitah mohaad griheetva asadgraahaan pravartante'shuchivrataah
translation: Given to insatiable passion, filled with hypocrisy, pride and arrogance, adopting vile objectives due to delusion, they work with impure resolves.
significance: The first stage of demoniacal behavior: passion, false objectives, and impure resolves.

text: chintaam aparimeyaam cha pralayaantaam upaashritaah kaamopabhoga-paramaa etaavad iti nishchitaah
translation: Beset with innumerable anxieties that end only with death, regarding the gratification of lust as the highest aim, and feeling certain that there is nothing else beyond it.
significance: Constant mental anxiety and obsession with material desires.

text: aashaa-paasha-shataih baddhaah kaama-krodha-paraayanaah eehante kaama-bhoga-artham anyaayena artha-sanchayaan
translation: Bound by a hundred shackles of hope, giving themselves wholly to passion and anger, they strive to accumulate wealth by unlawful means for sensual enjoyment.
significance: Greed and accumulation of wealth by illegal or immoral means.

text: idam adya mayaa labdham imam praapsye manoratham idam asti idam api me bhavishyanti punar dhanam
translation: They think: 'This has been gained by me today; I shall acquire this desired object tomorrow; this wealth is mine now, and this also shall be mine in future.'
significance: Self-centered egoism: obsession with ownership and future gains.

text: asau mayaa hatah shatruh hanishye cha aparaan api eeshvaro'ham aham bhogee siddho'ham balavaan sukhee
translation: They say: 'That enemy has been slain by me, and others also I shall destroy. I am the lord, I am the enjoyer, I am perfect, powerful and happy.'
significance: Aggressive dominance: delusions of omnipotence and control.

text: aadhyo'bhijanavaan asmi ko'nyo'sti sadrisho mayaa yakshye daasyaami modishye ity ajnaana-vimohitaah
translation: They brag: 'I am rich and high-born; who else is equal to me? I shall perform sacrifices, I shall give charity, I shall rejoice.' Thus they are deluded by ignorance.
significance: Pride of birth and status: superficial religious ostentation.

text: aneka-chitta-vibhraantaah moha-jaala-samaavritaah prasaktaah kaama-bhogeshu patanti narake'shuchau
translation: Bewildered by many a fanciful thought, entangled in the snare of delusion, and addicted to the gratification of lust, they fall into a foul hell.
significance: The ultimate destination of unchecked egoism: mental confusion and hellish states.

text: aatma-sambhaavitaah stabdhaah dhana-maana-mada-anvitah yajante naama-yajnaiste dambhena avidhi-poorvakam
translation: Self-conceited, stubborn, filled with pride and drunk with wealth, they perform sacrifices in name only, out of ostentation and contrary to scriptural ordinances.
significance: Hypocritical spiritual practices devoid of true devotion.

text: ahamkaaram balam darpam kaamam krodham cha samshritaah maam aatma-para-deheshu pradvisanto'bhyasuyakah
translation: Taking refuge in egoism, power, arrogance, passion, and anger, they hate Me in their own and others' bodies, becoming malicious by nature.
significance: Hating the divine presence that dwells within every living soul.

text: taan aham dvishatah kruraan samsaareshu naraadhamaan kshipaamy ajasram ashubhaan aasureeshu eva yonishu
translation: These cruel and hateful evil-doers, the lowest of humanity, I hurl repeatedly into the wombs of demons in the cycle of birth and death.
significance: Natural karmic selection: negative actions attract matching births.

text: aasureem yonim aapannaa moodhaah janmani janmani maam apraapyaiva kaunteya tato yaanti adhamaam gatim
translation: Entering the wombs of demons birth after birth, these deluded fools sink to lower states of existence without ever reaching Me, O son of Kunti.
significance: The downward spiritual spiral caused by uncorrected vices.

text: trividham narakasyedam dvaaram naashanam aatmanah kaamah krodhas tathaa lobhas tasmaad etat trayam tyajet
translation: There are three gates leading to this hell, destructive of the soul—Lust, Anger, and Greed. Therefore, one must abandon these three.
significance: The three gateway keys to hell: Lust (Kama), Anger (Krodha), and Greed (Lobha).

text: etaih vimuktah kaunteya tamo-dvaaraih tribhih narah aacharati aatmanah shreyas tato yaati paraam gatim
translation: A person who is liberated from these three gates to darkness, O son of Kunti, strives for the welfare of the soul and thereby attains the supreme goal.
significance: Turning towards the light and beginning self-improvement (Sadhana).

text: yah shaastra-vidhim utsrijya vartate kaama-kaaratah na sah siddhim avaapnoti na sukham na paraam gatim
translation: He who discards the ordinances of the scriptures and acts under the impulse of desire attains neither perfection, nor happiness, nor the supreme goal.
significance: The danger of ego-driven guidance over scriptural wisdom.

text: tasmaat shaastram pramaanum te kaaryaa-kaarya-vyavasthitau jnaatva shaastra-vidhaanoktam karma kartum iha arhasi
translation: Therefore, let the scriptures be your authority in determining what ought to be done and what ought not to be done. Guided by scriptural laws, you should perform your duty in this world.
significance: The scriptural ordinances as the ultimate guide to duty and action.
